title_guess: Brown Moss / Prees (plant-buying for the pond)

pages.text:
from before it was light. So after breakfast we decided to breakcamp & go home. We drove to Cambridge & visited the part of Kettles Yard that was open, & had a cup of coffee. Ollie Downes came to the buttery so that he could take in 2 guests (but didn't eat himself). The 5 of us had lunch. Our legs were soaking wet. We left [strikethrough: bottles] Peter's bike with William & drove home.

April 5th 2002 Friday
        At 6 we left to buy plants for the garden & new pond. Simon, Eve, [?that] Charles 12 & Tonic. Cold sunshine. Tim Noble put in the pond 4 weeks ago. He has a nursery at Prees north of Shrewsbury. The campsite is a mile south of Whitchurch. __Brown Moss__ * * *
__Saturday__. V. Cold in the night because I wasn't wearing a waist coat. Hips ached. [?Lovely] [?Sun] Cold. We went for a walk

around Brown Moss lake. V. pretty. little ponds, flags bright green. No great woods & old coppicing. Tonic enjoyed the walk. Simon had forgotten his gumboots so he & John shared where [?the] bridge was underwater.
        Off to Holly Farm nurseries. We bought Viburnum Burkwoodii, which has a gorgeous smell from long ago - chosen by John. Simon chose enormous bamboo. Eve bought red poppies. We are taking home plants - shrubs - for the mound behind the pond, which is absolutely bare. And we have asked for 50 hawthorn sticks to make a hedge along the cleavage. Tim spent ages with us. Then Helen gave us a lovely salad lunch. Another Tim arrived & we talked cars because he has a dubonnet coloured [strikethrough: [illegible]] Lotus Elise.
        We went on to Stapeley Water Gardens. They were rude at the entrance, watching people coming & going. We bought lilies & iris & oxygenators & 2 books on ponds. Simon &

Charles chose blue Shubrumpton, but we can't buy any until the plants have been in the pot for a month.
    We drove to Malpas church where there are apparently 2 fine statues, one pre-Ref, one post-Ref, but not only was the church locked but service times were posted just out of view. So we won't go to church there.
    Back in the van in the evening we again played Scrabble. John & Charles are keen on it.  has not only clean but shining
    Another cold night, aching lips. "showers
__Sunday__
    We wake to blue sky & sunshine. Charles was also very cold in the night. We reached Tim's nursery garden at 10 and loaded up the van with the plants. Then we went a short distance to Prees church. The kneelers are the most beautiful I've seen – each one a flower. Greens carefully controlled, fawn background, brown sides. Designed 20 years ago from pictures in the vicar's prayer book, by a man
